transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Rajan sir is not joining today — he has a client call. So let's keep this focused. I want to do a quick check first — Priya, yesterday's actions. Effort doc is in progress on my side, sharing by 6pm today as committed. Anything else pending from yesterday?

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: I confirmed the Wednesday client scope call — 11am. Rajan sir is joining. I've also prepared a preliminary agenda. That's done.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Good. Now — even before scope is finalized, I want to start thinking through the returns flow design. So that Wednesday we can speak confidently about the approach, not just the timeline.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Sure. So for refunds, we hit the payment gateway's refund endpoint. Standard flow. But Rahul — I've been thinking about this since yesterday. Remember on Day 3 we descoped the gateway-down fallback scenario? We said it's out of scope for Sprint 4?

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Yes.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: For the payment forward flow, descoping it was acceptable. If payment fails, user can try again. It's annoying but recoverable. But for refunds — if the gateway is down at the moment of refund and we have no fallback, the refund silently fails. Customer has returned the product but hasn't got their money back. That's a serious problem.

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: That's a customer support nightmare. They'll raise a dispute with the bank. Client will get chargebacks.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: So the async retry queue we didn't build in Sprint 4 — we actually need to build it now, for Sprint 5, before we even start returns flow.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Yes. And honestly — and I know this is uncomfortable to say — the 2 intermittent failures Neha saw in prod on Friday? The root cause is the same thing. We have no retry mechanism for payment forward either. We've been lucky that only 3 real transactions happened over the weekend and none hit the bad scenario.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Wait. So we have a known prod weakness in Sprint 4 that could cause a payment failure, AND Sprint 5 returns flow depends on fixing that same weakness first?

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: That's exactly correct.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Rahul this has to be in the document you're preparing for tomorrow. Clearly. Not buried. If something fails in prod and it comes out that we knew about this risk and didn't surface it formally — that is very bad for us.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Agreed. Suresh, let's write a technical risk document today alongside the effort doc. You write the technical detail — root cause, probability, impact. I'll write the business risk framing and proposed fix.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Yes. I'll have my part done by 4pm.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: For the returns UI — Rahul, is it a new page or part of the existing checkout flow? I need to know for my estimation.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Separate flow. User goes to order history, selects order, initiates return. Minimum 3 screens — order lookup, return reason selection, confirmation with refund amount.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: And admin dashboard — any mockup from client?

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Nothing. They said standard admin dashboard. Which tells us absolutely nothing.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: Okay. I'll put together a reference list of what standard admin dashboard usually means — user management, order overview, payment reports, basic analytics. We confirm scope in Wednesday's call.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Good. Everyone please review the effort doc when I share tonight. Wednesday's call is important — we need to go in aligned.
